# @vllnt/convex-metering

> Metered usage records — idempotent usage events rolled up per billing period, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-metering` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`. It stores
four sandboxed tables — `meters` (definition: an `aggregation` rule of `sum`/`max`/`last` + display
`unit`, locked once usage exists), `records` (append-only raw usage events + optional `actorRef`),
`seen` (the idempotency-key ledger, **separate from `records`** so pruning audit never re-opens a
duplicate), and `rollups` (the materialized per-`(meter, subject, period)` aggregate, the O(1)
billable truth, with `closedAt` to freeze a period). `record` folds a usage event into the rollup and
is idempotent via `idempotencyKey` (sum meters only; a key on a gauge throws); `recordWithLimit`
atomically refuses to cross a `limit`; `adjust` posts signed corrections to sum meters (never below
zero); `closePeriod` freezes a billed period (later writes throw `PERIOD_CLOSED`). `usage`/`listUsage`/
`listSubjectUsage` read rollups (with `closed`); `verify` reconciles a rollup against surviving
records; `reset` (one period) and `eraseSubject` (GDPR, all meters) delete in self-rescheduling
batches; `pruneRecords` and `pruneSeen` retain records and dedup keys on independent cutoffs. `period`
is an opaque host string (no date parsing); record integer minor-units for money precision; time is
server-sourced. The component owns accurate, idempotent usage accounting; the host owns the meter
meaning, the opaque `subjectRef`/`actorRef`, period bucketing, pricing, precision, retention, and auth.

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
